# InitiatorAddr

Identifies the initiator of a transaction - either by public key or account hash.

## Import

```ts
import { InitiatorAddr } from 'casper-js-sdk';
```

## Class

### `InitiatorAddr`

| Property | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `publicKey?` | `PublicKey` | Initiator's public key |
| `accountHash?` | `AccountHash` | Initiator's account hash |

#### Constructor

```ts
new InitiatorAddr(publicKey?: PublicKey, accountHash?: AccountHash)
```

#### Methods

```ts
addr.toBytes(): Uint8Array
addr.toJSON(): unknown
InitiatorAddr.fromJSON(json: any): InitiatorAddr
```

## Usage

```ts
import { InitiatorAddr, PublicKey } from 'casper-js-sdk';

// From public key
const addr = new InitiatorAddr(somePublicKey);

// From JSON (as returned by RPC)
const addr2 = InitiatorAddr.fromJSON({
  PublicKey: '01abc...'
});

// Or account hash
const addr3 = InitiatorAddr.fromJSON({
  AccountHash: 'account-hash-abc...'
});

// Check which type it is
if (addr2.publicKey) {
  console.log('Public key initiator:', addr2.publicKey.toHex());
} else if (addr2.accountHash) {
  console.log('Account hash initiator:', addr2.accountHash.toHex());
}
```

## See Also

- [`Transaction`](/types/transaction) - Uses `InitiatorAddr`
- [`ExecutionResult`](/types/execution-result) - Contains `initiator: InitiatorAddr`
- [`TransactionV1Payload`](/types/transaction-v1-payload) - Payload field
